properties.evidence: The text mentions that maximal therapeutic doses of lipid lowering agents can have very high cholesterol levels (> 300 mg/dL), very high TG levels (>500 mg/dL) as adverse effects.
The text mentions that very high cholesterol levels (> 300 mg/dL) and very high TG levels (>500 mg/dL) can cause xanthomas.

rejected_candidates.text: Although rare in primary care practices, many genetic causes of hyperlipidemia carry an increased risk of premature CVD and/or other organ system disease.
rejected_candidates.reason: The text does not explicitly support a direct causal relationship between genetic causes of hyperlipidemia and CVD or organ system disease. It only mentions that these syndromes require different treatment.

rejected_candidates.text: In general, the clues to a genetic syndrome include very high cholesterol levels (> 300 mg/dL), very high TG levels (>500 mg/dL), xanthomas, strong family history of hyperlipidemia or early CVD, or lack of expected response to maximal therapeutic doses of lipid lowering agents.
rejected_candidates.reason: The text does not support a direct causal relationship between the genetic causes and conditions mentioned. It only provides clues for considering a lipid specialty referral.
